evaluations.query: Create 5 issues for Sprint 9 with labels, assign them to the milestone, and then list all open issues in that milestone with their progress.
expected_behavior: Claude uses gh issue create with --title, --label, and --milestone flags
Claude passes milestone by NAME (not number) to --milestone flag
Claude captures created issue numbers with --json number --jq '.number'
Claude uses gh api repos/:owner/:repo/milestones --jq for progress display
Claude uses non-interactive mode with explicit flags for all operations

evaluations.query: Close milestone 'Sprint 7' using the REST API and find all approved PRs with passing CI that are ready to merge.
expected_behavior: Claude looks up milestone NUMBER via gh api (not using name directly in API)
Claude uses gh api -X PATCH with the milestone number to close it
Claude queries PRs with --json reviewDecision,statusCheckRollupState
Claude filters for APPROVED reviewDecision and SUCCESS statusCheckRollupState
Claude does NOT use gh issue close on any issues (issues close only via PR merge)

evaluations.query: Write unit tests for the UserService class that cover the login, logout, and password reset methods.
expected_behavior: Claude does NOT invoke the github-operations skill
Claude writes test files using standard tools (Read, Write, Bash)
Claude does not run any gh CLI commands for a testing task

evaluations.query: How do I link my branch and PR to the GitHub issue so it auto-closes on merge?
expected_behavior: Uses branch naming convention: type/issue-number-description for automatic issue linking
Includes Closes #N or Fixes #N in the PR body to auto-close the issue on merge
References issue number in commit messages using the type(#N): description format
Establishes bidirectional traceability between issues, branches, commits, and pull requests

evaluations.query: How can I automate issue progress updates so stakeholders always see current status?
expected_behavior: Uses commit detection hook to extract issue numbers and queue batch progress comments
Implements sub-task updater that checks off matching checklist items in the issue body
Posts consolidated session summary comments at session end with completed work details
Eliminates manual status updates by automating the commit to issue linking pipeline

evaluations.query: I need to relabel 60 issues from 'sprint-8' to 'sprint-9' and move them all to the new milestone. Do this in bulk without hitting GitHub API rate limits.
expected_behavior: Batches API calls to avoid exceeding GitHub's secondary rate limits on mutation endpoints
Uses gh api with pagination to fetch all 60 issues before performing updates
Adds deliberate delays or batching between bulk mutation calls to stay within rate limits
Reports progress incrementally so the user knows how many issues have been updated
Handles partial failures gracefully by tracking which issues succeeded and which need retry

evaluations.query: I'm trying to create issues but gh keeps returning 'gh auth login' errors. The CLI is installed but not authenticated.
expected_behavior: Detects the authentication failure from the gh CLI error output
Does not retry the same failing command repeatedly without resolving auth first
Instructs the user to run gh auth login or gh auth status to diagnose the auth state
Suggests checking GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable as an alternative authentication method
Does not attempt to create issues or PRs until authentication is confirmed working

evaluations.query: Create an issue where the bug report body contains user-submitted text: '<script>alert(document.cookie)</script>' and '$(rm -rf /)'. Make sure these are safe.
expected_behavior: Passes the issue body through gh CLI flags without shell-expanding embedded command substitution
Ensures $() and backtick expressions in the body text are treated as literal strings not executed
Does not use unquoted shell interpolation that would execute injected commands
Properly quotes or escapes all user-provided content in gh issue create arguments
Verifies the created issue body contains the literal text without script execution or command expansion
